body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` **Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` **Triggered by:** d2dee26410b69500a2533cdae873a2ec18655541 (#56911) by @MaiLinhP ## What Changed PR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration brought in (or confirmed the presence of) two new hand-authored clients — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — as part of the Unified Messaging feature set first introduced in 1.3.0-beta.1. Custom suppression of `BuilderExtension` and `ClientSettings` was also added for types using `CommunicationTokenCredential` to preserve AOT compatibility. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` covers only the legacy WhatsApp-specific notification path (`NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`). The new **Unified Messaging** surface — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — is completely undocumented. - No examples show how to authenticate or instantiate `ConversationAdministrationClient` or `ConversationThreadClient`, including via `CommunicationTokenCredential` (whose `BuilderExtension`/`ClientSettings` were explicitly customised in this PR). - `ConversationThreadClientSettings` (new custom type added in this PR) is not mentioned anywhere in user-facing docs. - The `## Key concepts` section is a collapsed sub-heading inside `## Getting started` rather than a standalone `## Key concepts` H2 section, and its content is a single line that does not describe the two new clients. - The CHANGELOG `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is empty; the generator migration and new client-settings type should be acknowledged there. - No sample test file (`tests/Samples/`) exists for the Unified Messaging APIs (conversations), so there are no snippet sources for the README.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md` Add entries under the `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` header: ```markdown ## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated library to new TypeSpec-based emitter. - Added `ConversationThreadClientSettings` to support configuring `ConversationThreadClient` with `CommunicationTokenCredential`. ``` #### 1b. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` Restructure the README to comply with the standard template. Key changes: 1. Move `### Key concepts` out of `## Getting started` and make it a top-level `## Key concepts` H2 section. 2. Expand **Key concepts** to describe all four clients: `NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`, `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`. 3. Add a new **Authenticate with CommunicationTokenCredential** sub-section showing how to create a `ConversationThreadClient` with a token credential. 4. Add new **Examples** sub-sections for Unified Messaging: creating a conversation, listing conversations, sending/receiving messages on a conversation thread. Suggested **Key concepts** section content: ```markdown ## Key concepts - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends outbound notification messages (WhatsApp, SMS). - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves approved message templates. - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (Unified Messaging). - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (Unified Messaging). ``` Suggested **Authenticate the client** addition (append after the existing connection-string example): ```markdown #### CommunicationTokenCredential `ConversationThreadClient` also supports authentication via `CommunicationTokenCredential`: ```C# Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at: `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationMessaging.cs` The file should: - Include `[Test]` (NUnit) methods, one per snippet. - Wrap snippet code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. Snippets to implement: **Snippet: `CreateConversationAdministrationClient`** ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient var connectionString = ""; ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken`** (referenced in README above) ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken var endpoint = new Uri(""); var tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); var settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings("", endpoint, tokenCredential); ConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings); #endregion ``` **Snippet: `ListConversations`** ```csharp #region Snippet:ListConversations AsyncPageable conversations = adminClient.GetConversationsAsync(); await foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations) { Console.WriteLine($"Conversation id: {conversation.Id}"); } #endregion ``` Add README placeholders at the appropriate locations: ``` ```C# Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient ``` ``` ```C# Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken ``` ``` ```C# Snippet:ListConversations ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections as top-level H2 (`##`) headings in this order: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but `Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside it 2. ❌ **Key concepts** — must be moved to its own `##` section and expanded to cover all four clients 3. ✅ **Examples** — present but missing Unified Messaging examples 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — confirm the README is updated with the injected snippet bodies 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — regenerates API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Communication] Docs: Update README to document Unified Messaging APIs (ConversationAdministrationClient, ConversationThreadClient)
